microsoftSourceClaims.claimText: Microsoft renamed the Power BI dataset content type to semantic model, so current documentation and human-readable output use semantic model terminology.
Power BI row-level security restricts data access only for users with the workspace Viewer role and does not apply to workspace Admin, Member, or Contributor roles.
Power BI workspaces expose the Admin, Member, Contributor, and Viewer roles, and Viewer is the least-privilege read-only role for report consumers.
Sharing Power BI content requires a Power BI Pro or Premium Per User license unless the content resides in Premium or Fabric capacity, which report authors and workspace administrators must hold.

Groups - Get Groups (GET /v1.0/myorg/groups) returns workspaces accessible to the caller and requires Workspace.Read.All or Workspace.ReadWrite.All. This first lab cycle uses preauthorized delegated Workspace.Read.All only.
Tenant-wide Power BI and Fabric settings are managed by the Fabric administrator role (formerly the Power BI administrator role) in the admin portal.

licenses: Power BI Pro or Premium Per User license, or Viewer access to content in Power BI Premium or Fabric capacity
roles: Power BI workspace Viewer (read-only) on an existing governance dashboard workspace
Global Reader for tenant identity and license inspection
features: An existing Power BI/Fabric workspace containing a report and a semantic model
Preauthorized delegated Workspace.Read.All scope for GET /v1.0/myorg/groups (no Workspace.ReadWrite.All in this cycle)
capacityRequirements: Read-only access to an existing workspace and capacity; no new capacity assignment
dataRequirements: Representative sample evidence only; no production tenant rows, PBIX/PBIP content, or upstream evidence retained
prerequisites.notes: This first cycle is read-only and detect-only. It uses preauthorized delegated Workspace.Read.All only for GET /v1.0/myorg/groups; no Workspace.ReadWrite.All, no service principal tenant-setting changes, and no consent requests are permitted. Missing scope or consent is recorded as an evidence-backed BLOCKED disposition. Where a workspace, report, semantic model, license, capacity, role, or upstream evidence package is unavailable, the reviewer records an evidence-backed BLOCKED or NOT-APPLICABLE disposition rather than proceeding.

steps.intent: Positively confirm the tenant and target Power BI/Fabric workspace identity for the review without copying raw identifiers.
steps.expected: Tenant and workspace identity are confirmed; only a non-reversible reference such as a truncated hash is recorded.
steps.locator: app.powerbi.com > Workspaces > target governance workspace > Settings/About

readBack.expectation: Identity is confirmed and no raw tenant, workspace, or user identifier is retained.

steps.intent: Confirm licensing, least-privilege Viewer role, capacity prerequisites, and preauthorized delegated Workspace.Read.All scope are present without modifying them.
steps.expected: Prerequisites are present, or a documented gap is recorded as an evidence-backed BLOCKED or NOT-APPLICABLE disposition. Missing Workspace.Read.All scope or missing consent evidence is recorded as BLOCKED.
steps.operatorNote: Confirm Power BI Pro/PPU or Fabric/Premium capacity licensing, Viewer (read-only) role on the target workspace, and preauthorized delegated Workspace.Read.All for GET /v1.0/myorg/groups. Do not assign Fabric administrator for this lab, do not request consent, and do not use Workspace.ReadWrite.All. If scope/consent evidence is missing, record BLOCKED.

readBack.expectation: Prerequisite state is recorded, scope/consent status is attested, Fabric administrator is documented as not required for Viewer inspection, and no license, role, capacity, tenant setting, or consent state was changed.

phases.title: Read-only inspection and representative script runs

steps.intent: List the Power BI/Fabric workspaces the reviewer can access, read-only, to confirm an existing governance workspace is present.
steps.expected: A read-only workspace list is returned from GET /v1.0/myorg/groups; only aggregate count and boolean target-workspace-match are recorded, not workspace names, IDs, or URLs. Missing Workspace.Read.All scope or missing consent evidence is recorded as BLOCKED.

readBack.reference: Power BI REST API groups response
readBack.expectation: Response confirms workspace accessibility with aggregate count and target-workspace-match boolean only; no workspace IDs, names, or URLs are retained.

steps.intent: Use Viewer-only access to render and interact with report pages and confirm workspace/report/semantic-model item existence and metadata without edit permissions.
steps.expected: Viewer confirms report-page behavior and item existence/metadata. Viewer does not inspect model-view tables/measures directly and this cycle does not require Build permission or edit roles. Documented tables/measures are verified against repository config/docs and representative feed artifacts. Live schema metadata is optional only when a preauthorized read-only metadata surface exists, with no underlying rows exported. Missing access is recorded as BLOCKED or NOT-APPLICABLE.
steps.locator: app.powerbi.com > target workspace > report pages and semantic model item metadata

readBack.reference: Report page interaction and item metadata view
readBack.expectation: Viewer-level read-back confirms report interactivity and item existence/metadata only; Build permission or edit role is not required; no data rows, PBIX/PBIP content, or identifiers are exported or retained.

steps.intent: Run the representative feed and evidence scripts in documentation-first mode.
steps.expected: Scripts emit seeded artifacts, SHA-256 companions, and documentation-first runtime markers with an explicit data-quality gap for unresolved freshness.
steps.command: pwsh -File solutions/12-regulatory-compliance-dashboard/scripts/Export-Evidence.ps1 -ConfigurationTier recommended -OutputPath lab-output; pwsh -File solutions/12-regulatory-compliance-dashboard/scripts/Monitor-Compliance.ps1 -ConfigurationTier recommended -OutputPath lab-output

readBack.expectation: Output includes runtime-mode markers, SHA-256 companions, and a data-quality gap rather than current freshness.

phases.title: Validate schema, hashes, freshness, and source currency

steps.intent: Validate the emitted evidence against the shared schema, verify SHA-256 hash lineage, and confirm freshness and provenance and that control status stays separate from lab disposition.
steps.expected: Every artifact has a matching SHA-256 companion, the package validates against data/evidence-schema.json, freshness reports an explicit gap rather than current, and control statuses are not converted into lab dispositions.

readBack.expectation: Schema validation passes, hashes match, freshness shows a gap, and control-status versus lab-disposition separation holds.

steps.intent: Confirm the Microsoft Learn source citations remain current and match the recorded lifecycle state.
steps.expected: Each cited source remains published and generally available, or a change is recorded as a finding.
steps.operatorNote: Confirm the cited Microsoft Learn pages (semantic model terminology, RLS Viewer-only scope, workspace roles, licensing, Groups - Get Groups GET /v1.0/myorg/groups with required scopes, and Fabric admin role boundaries) remain published and match the recorded lifecycle state.

readBack.expectation: Sources remain current and support the documented Power BI and Fabric claims.

steps.intent: Capture operator attestation that no mutable changes were made and no sensitive identifiers were retained.
steps.expected: Attestation confirms a read-only cycle with no retained sensitive data.
steps.operatorNote: Attest that the cycle was read-only and detect-only, that no publish, refresh, share, role, RLS/OLS, gateway, capacity, tenant-setting, service-principal-setting, or consent changes occurred, and that no raw identifiers, URLs, rows, PBIX/PBIP content, tokens, or credentials were retained.

readBack.expectation: Attestation confirms no mutation occurred and no sensitive identifiers were retained.

requiredArtifacts.description: PowerShell transcript and read-back output from the representative feed and evidence script runs, including runtime-mode markers and the data-quality gap.
Microsoft Learn source citations confirming current Power BI and Fabric terminology, RLS Viewer-only scope, workspace roles, licensing, and Groups - Get Groups scope requirements.

Attestation of Viewer-level read-only workspace/report/semantic-model item inspection with aggregate counts and boolean workspace-match only from Groups response, no model-view table/measure inspection requirement, and no raw tenant/workspace/report/semantic-model identifiers retained.
